purpose: Candidate pool for later HEI canonical batches while terminal access is unavailable. These are not canonical records yet. Each candidate needs a final duplicate check and public-quality staging package before append.

status_legend.A: Likely HEI-addable after one more source/duplicate pass
status_legend.B: Potential event/update candidate, usually existing entity or service-level incident
status_legend.C: Hold / weak / likely non-HEI unless better evidence appears

candidates.headline: Reports of Arkham Exchange shutdown were denied; Arkham said the exchange was pivoting from a centralized model to a fully decentralized DEX model.
candidates.hei_relevance: Useful as a rebrand/pivot/service-model-change record, but not a dead record. Treat as limited/pivot unless stronger evidence shows CEX closure without continuity.

duplicate_check.result: no existing repository hit via connector search

candidates.next_action: Confirm whether HEI should model Arkham as hybrid limited / rebranded / active with service pivot. Need one additional source or official Arkham statement before canonical insertion.

candidates.headline: Q1 2026 shutdown roundups list Slingshot, described as a DeFi trade aggregator, among projects that shut down amid lower volumes and funding pressure.
candidates.hei_relevance: Potential HEI target only if Slingshot is treated as a trading/DEX aggregator rather than a generic DeFi tool. Needs direct team notice or official shutdown page before public-quality canonical insertion.

duplicate_check.result: no existing repository hit via connector search

candidates.next_action: Find direct Slingshot team announcement. If found, stage as dead / voluntary_shutdown / medium. If not found, keep as watchlist only.

candidates.headline: CoinDCX confirmed a July 2025 security breach in an internal operational account, with about USD 44 million stolen; reports say customer funds were not affected and withdrawals later resumed.
candidates.hei_relevance: Good active-side CEX incident candidate: hack/security breach, temporary service impact or Web3 pause, later restoration. Not a dead record.

duplicate_check.result: no existing repository hit via connector search

candidates.next_action: Stage as active CEX with hack/security_breach and restoration/withdrawal-resumed events. Use careful wording that internal operational funds, not customer funds, were reported affected.

candidates.known_entity_hint: Bybit is already in canonical data as hei_ex_000011 from the prior regional-exit batch.
candidates.headline: Bybit suffered a February 2025 Ethereum wallet hack widely reported as about USD 1.4-1.5 billion, then closed the ETH gap / restored 1:1 backing.
candidates.hei_relevance: Very strong active-side CEX incident update for an existing entity. Not a shutdown, but one of the largest exchange theft events and therefore material to HEI history.

duplicate_check.result: existing canonical Bybit entity expected; add events/evidence only

candidates.next_action: Stage as existing Bybit event update: hack, withdrawal_pressure/operational_stress if supported by source, reserve_gap_closed/recovery as other. Keep entity active.

candidates.headline: Reports described a post-shutdown user-data leak involving FTX Japan / Custodiem infrastructure.
candidates.hei_relevance: Negative and exchange-related, but not a closure, hack of exchange funds, or current exchange-status change. Only add if HEI decides to track post-mortem data-leak events for dead exchanges.
candidates.next_action: Hold unless user wants post-shutdown security/privacy incidents included in HEI.

next_batch_recommendation.reason: CoinDCX and Bybit are strong CEX incident records with multiple good sources. Arkham and Slingshot need direct/official confirmation or clearer HEI modeling. FTX Japan data leak is exchange-related but may be outside the core HEI event scope.
